response: This Python function implements the classic Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm, an efficient number-theoretic method to find all prime numbers less than or equal to a given input integer n. It initializes a boolean array to track primality, iterates only up to the square root of n to mark all multiples of each confirmed prime (starting from the prime's square to avoid redundant calculations) as non-prime, then returns a list of all indices that remain flagged as prime.
